primary_factors: No snow/precipitation signal and no winter alerts; primary roads expected clear by Monday morning
Temperatures near-freezing around daybreak but warming after sunrise; wind chills in the 20s (no extreme-cold closure trigger)
Main risk is *localized* refreeze/black ice on bridges/overpasses and shaded rural roads after weekend rain—typically mitigated by spot-salting and driver caution
No neighboring-district closure/delay signals found; no community “closure buzz” or district ‘closings watch’ messaging at this time
Power grid risk appears low with no evidence of storm-scale outages; any issues would likely be isolated
decision_rationale: All experts broadly agree this is an “open” setup for Rockford on Mon 2026-03-09, and there is no credible wind-chill trigger (web verification explicitly found no ≤ -20°F values; meteorologist expects wind chills in the 20s). The only meaningful risk pathway is a narrow early-morning refreeze/black-ice window after a wet Fri–Sat, but with no ongoing precipitation, clear major routes, and typical spot-treatment capability, this rarely produces a district-wide closure in West Michigan. News intelligence found no neighboring-district closures/delays and no Rockford ‘closings watch’ messaging, which reinforces the low-probability call. Infrastructure context indicates no plow event is needed (no snow), and any response would be targeted salting of bridge decks/known trouble spots. Power-grid review shows no indication of a widespread outage event that would force closure. The main uncertainty is forecast-date alignment (some data centered on Sun 3/8 rather than Mon 3/9), so I’m not going to 0%, but the Michigan-appropriate Brier-optimized stance is decisively low. Recommendation: OPEN, with early AM monitoring for patchy black ice; a district-wide delay/closure would require evidence of widespread icing (numerous crashes, confirmed subfreezing persistence, or freezing drizzle), which is not currently supported.

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 1 round(s) with 1.0% spread. Average probability: 2%.
